They're Emancipated.


AS the discovery and use of gum
powder placed the peasant upon an
equal war footing with the mailed
and helmeted knight, so the enor-
nous increase in mechanical appli-
taken woman out of the ranks of
the unpaid menials and placed her on
an equality with man in the war;
fare for existence. The commercial
evolution that is going on in the
world around us has given her OPP
portunity to obtain recompense for
toll, leisure for mental development,
and is slowly but surely breaking
down the physical barriers that had
hitherto been considered insurmount-
able.


From the last censue we learn
that there are now in this country
between 100000 and 100000 wom-
en- and girls earning their own liv-
lihood. The rapidly increasing num-
bers of breadwinners among women
makes it painfully eviden that men
are unable, unwilling or incompetent
to support women, that indeed they
are not capable of supporting them
selves, as evidenced by the Dread
line" around Fleischman's bakery dur
ing the winter months, and by statis-
tics that there are $4,000 men in New
York city who are supported by
women.


Demands Coming.


AS women wage earners are with
out exception paid smaller salary

the accident of sex.


But here it is well to emphasize
the fact that, despite the utterances
Of the childless women who are
leading the suffrage movement. We
cannot violate or ignore with i'm
punity the laws of nature, for they
